name: Remote Code Execution (RCE)
template:
## Title
Remote Code Execution via [injection point]

## Severity
Critical (CVSS 9.8)

## Description
[Application] is vulnerable to remote code execution through [vector]. An attacker can execute arbitrary commands on the server by [method].

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Navigate to [URL]
2. [Action that triggers the vulnerability]
3. Inject the following payload: `[payload]`
4. Observe command execution in the response

## Proof of Concept
```
[PoC code/request]
```

## Impact
- Full server compromise
- Access to sensitive data
- Lateral movement within network
- Data exfiltration

## Remediation
- Sanitize all user input
- Use parameterized commands
- Implement WAF rules
- Apply principle of least privilege

## References
- CWE-78: OS Command Injection
- OWASP Command Injection

name: SQL Injection
template:
## Title
SQL Injection in [parameter/endpoint]

## Severity
Critical (CVSS 9.1)

## Description
The [parameter] on [endpoint] is vulnerable to SQL injection. An attacker can extract, modify, or delete database contents.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Navigate to [URL]
2. Enter the following in the [field]: `' OR 1=1--`
3. Observe the modified query response
4. Use UNION SELECT to extract data

## Proof of Concept
```
GET /api/users?id=1' UNION SELECT username,password FROM users--
```

## Impact
- Full database access
- Authentication bypass
- Data theft (PII, credentials)
- Potential RCE via SQL features

## Remediation
- Use parameterized queries/prepared statements
- Implement input validation
- Apply least-privilege database accounts
- Enable WAF SQL injection rules

name: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
template: ## Title [Stored/Reflected/DOM] XSS in [feature] ## Severity High (CVSS 7.5) ## Description A [stored/reflected/DOM-based] cross-site scripting vulnerability exists in [feature]. User-supplied input is rendered without proper sanitization. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Navigate to [URL] 2. Enter the following payload: `` 3. [Submit/view the page] 4. Observe JavaScript execution ## Proof of Concept ``` POST /api/comment {"body": ""} ``` ## Impact - Session hijacking - Account takeover - Credential theft - Malware distribution ## Remediation - Output encode all user-supplied data - Implement Content Security Policy - Use HttpOnly and Secure cookie flags

name: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
template:
## Title
SSRF via [feature]

## Severity
High (CVSS 8.6)

## Description
The [feature] allows server-side requests to arbitrary URLs. An attacker can access internal services, cloud metadata, and perform port scanning.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Navigate to [URL]
2. Set the URL parameter to: `http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/`
3. Observe internal data in response

## Impact
- Cloud credential theft
- Internal network scanning
- Access to internal services

## Remediation
- Whitelist allowed destinations
- Block private IP ranges and metadata endpoints
- Use a proxy for outbound requests

name: Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR)
template:
## Title
IDOR in [endpoint] Exposes [resource]

## Severity
High (CVSS 7.8)

## Description
The API endpoint [path] does not verify resource ownership. By modifying the [id parameter], an attacker can access other users' [data type].

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Authenticate as User A
2. Access: `GET /api/users/123/documents`
3. Change ID: `GET /api/users/124/documents`
4. Observe User B's documents returned

## Impact
- Unauthorized data access
- Privacy violation
- Potential data modification

## Remediation
- Implement authorization checks on every request
- Use UUIDs instead of sequential IDs
- Verify resource ownership server-side

name: Authentication Bypass
template:
## Title
Authentication Bypass via [method]

## Severity
Critical (CVSS 9.4)

## Description
The authentication mechanism can be bypassed by [technique]. This allows an attacker to access authenticated functionality without valid credentials.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Navigate to login page
2. [Bypass technique details]
3. Access the authenticated dashboard

## Impact
- Complete authentication bypass
- Access to all user accounts
- Administrative access

## Remediation
- Fix the authentication logic
- Implement multi-factor authentication
- Add brute-force protection

name: CSRF (Cross-Site Request Forgery)
template:
## Title
CSRF on [action] Endpoint

## Severity
Medium (CVSS 6.5)

## Description
The [action] endpoint lacks CSRF protection. An attacker can forge requests to perform [action] on behalf of authenticated users.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Create HTML page with auto-submitting form targeting [endpoint]
2. Host page on attacker server
3. Send link to authenticated victim
4. Action executes without victim's consent

## PoC HTML
```html
<form action='https://target.com/api/change-email' method='POST'>
  <input name='email' value='attacker@evil.com'>
</form>
<script>document.forms[0].submit()</script>
```

## Remediation
- Implement CSRF tokens
- Use SameSite cookie attribute
- Verify Origin/Referer headers

name: Information Disclosure
template:
## Title
Sensitive Information Disclosure via [source]

## Severity
Medium (CVSS 5.3)

## Description
Sensitive information is exposed through [source]. This includes [type of data] which could aid further attacks.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Navigate to [URL]
2. Observe sensitive data in [response/source code/headers]

## Data Exposed
- [List of sensitive data found]

## Remediation
- Remove debug information from production
- Disable directory listing
- Review error handling

name: Broken Access Control
template:
## Title
Broken Access Control - [specific issue]

## Severity
High (CVSS 8.0)

## Description
Access control checks are missing/insufficient on [endpoint], allowing [low-privilege role] to perform [high-privilege action].

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Log in as [low-privilege user]
2. Send request to [admin endpoint]
3. Observe the action succeeds

## Impact
- Privilege escalation
- Unauthorized admin actions
- Data manipulation

## Remediation
- Implement role-based access control
- Verify permissions server-side
- Log and monitor privilege usage

name: Race Condition
template:
## Title
Race Condition in [feature]

## Severity
High (CVSS 7.5)

## Description
A race condition exists in [feature] that allows [abuse]. By sending concurrent requests, an attacker can [impact].

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Prepare [action]
2. Send 20 concurrent requests using:
```bash
for i in $(seq 1 20); do curl -X POST [endpoint] & done; wait
```
3. Observe multiple successful actions

## Impact
- [Financial/logical impact]
- Bypass rate limits
- Duplicate transactions

## Remediation
- Implement database-level locking
- Use idempotency keys
- Add mutex/semaphore controls
